title: Fibonacci partial-sum identity
informal_statement: The sum of the first n Fibonacci numbers (fib 0 + fib 1 + ... + fib (n-1)) plus 1 equals fib (n+1): (sum_{i<n} fib i) + 1 = fib(n+1). Phrased additively (sum + 1 = fib(n+1) rather than sum = fib(n+1) - 1) to avoid Nat truncated subtraction.
informal_proof_idea: Induction on n. Base case n = 0: the empty sum is 0, and 0 + 1 = 1 = fib 1 by definition. Successor case: given ih : (sum_{i<k} fib i) + 1 = fib(k+1), split off the last term via Finset.sum_range_succ to get (sum_{i<k} fib i + fib k) + 1, rewrite fib(k+2) via Nat.fib_add_two (fib(k+2) = fib k + fib(k+1)) on the goal's right side, then omega closes the resulting linear rearrangement using ih and the two rewritten atoms.
formal_statement_pp: theorem fib_sum_succ : ∀ n : ℕ, (∑ i ∈ Finset.range n, Nat.fib i) + 1 = Nat.fib (n + 1)

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode fe47cf48), kernel_verified on the first Solve attempt. Distinct from the already-authored elementary.induction.fib_le_two_pow.v1 (a growth-rate bound on a hand-rolled fib defined via SubmitModule pair-recursion): this packet uses Mathlib's Nat.fib directly and proves an exact partial-sum identity rather than an inequality. Picked as a fresh target after induction's QUEUE.md Next targets/Backlog were both empty this cycle (repopulated from the domain's LOOP.md focus list: finite sums/products, recurrence relations).
